label: legend | be | first
sentence:
Legend of the Corn

The Arikara were the first to find the maize.

label: man | go | ∅
sentence: A young man went out hunting.

label: he | come | hill
sentence: He came to a high hill.

label: he | see | bull
sentence: Looking down a valley, he saw a buffalo bull near where two rivers joined.

label: he | see | ∅
sentence: When the young man looked to see how he could kill the buffalo, he saw how beautiful the country was.

label: bank | be | tree
sentence: The banks of the two rivers were low, with many trees.

label: he | get | bowshot
sentence: The buffalo faced the north; therefore he could not get within bowshot of him.

label: he | think | ∅
sentence: He thought he should wait until the buffalo moved close to the banks of one of the rivers, or to a ravine where there were bushes and shrubs.

label: man | wait | ∅
sentence: So the young man waited.

label: sun | go | ∅
sentence: The sun went down before the buffalo moved.

label: hunter | lie | ∅
sentence: Nearly all night the hunter lay awake.

label: he | have | food
sentence: He had little food.

label: he | feel | ∅
sentence: He felt sorry he could not reach the buffalo.

label: he | hurry | top
sentence: Before the sun rose, he hurried to the top of the hill.

label: buffalo | stand | ∅
sentence: The buffalo stood just where it had, but it faced the east.

label: he | wait | ∅
sentence: Again, he waited for it to move.

label: he | wait | ∅
sentence: He waited all day.

label: buffalo | stand | place
sentence: When the sun went down, the buffalo still stood in the same place.

label: man | lie | ∅
sentence: Nearly all night the young man lay awake.

label: he | have | food
sentence: He had very little food indeed.

label: he | rise | ∅
sentence: The next morning, he rose early, and came to the top of the hill, just as the sun came up.

label: buffalo | stand | place
sentence: The buffalo was still standing in the same place; but now it faced the south.

label: he | wait | ∅
sentence: He waited all day.

label: sun | go | ∅
sentence: Then the sun went down.

label: it | face | west
sentence: Now the next morning, when he arose early, the buffalo stood in the same place; this time it faced the west.

label: man | wait | ∅
sentence: All day the young man waited, but the buffalo did not move.

label: man | think | ∅
sentence: Now the young man thought, “Why does not the buffalo move?”

label: ∅ | sleep | ∅
sentence: He saw it did not drink, did not eat, did not sleep.

label: he | think | ∅
sentence: He thought some power must be influencing it.

label: man | hurry | top
sentence: Now the next morning, the young man hurried to the top of the hill.

label: sun | rise | ∅
sentence: The sun had risen and everything was light.

label: buffalo | be | ∅
sentence: The buffalo was gone.

label: he | see | ∅
sentence: Then he saw where the buffalo had stood there was a strange bush.

label: he | see | ∅
sentence: He went to the place; then he saw it was a plant.

label: he | look | track
sentence: He looked for the tracks of the buffalo.

label: he | see | ∅
sentence: He saw where it had turned to the east and to the south and to the west.

label: plant | grow | ∅
sentence: In the center there was one track; out of it the small plant had grown.

label: ∅ | be | track
sentence: There was no track to show where the buffalo had left the place.

label: hunter | hurry | village
sentence: Then the hunter hurried to his village.

label: he | tell | chief
sentence: He told the chiefs and the people of the strange buffalo and the plant.

label: chief | come | place
sentence: So, all the chiefs and the people came to the place.

label: they | see | track
sentence: They saw the tracks of the buffalo as he had stood, but there were no tracks of his coming or going.

label: people | know | ∅
sentence: So, all the people knew that Wahkoda had given this strange plant to the people.

label: they | know | plant
sentence: They knew of other plants they might eat.

label: they | know | ∅
sentence: They knew there was a time when each plant was ripe.

label: they | guard | it
sentence: So, they watched the strange plant; they guarded it and protected it.

label: flower | appear | plant
sentence: Then a flower appeared on the plant.

label: part | push | one
sentence: Afterwards, at one of the joints, a new part of the plant pushed out.

label: it | have | hair
sentence: It had hair.

label: it | be | ∅
sentence: At first the hair was green; then it was brown.

label: people | think | ∅
sentence: Then the people thought, “Perhaps this fruit is ripe.”

label: they | dare | ∅
sentence: But they did not dare touch it.

label: they | meet | ∅
sentence: They met together.

label: they | look | plant
sentence: They looked at the plant.

label: man | say | ∅
sentence: Then a young man said, “My life has not been good.

label: it | matter | ∅
sentence: If any evil comes to me, it will not matter.”

label: people | be | ∅
sentence: So the people were willing, and the young man put his hand on the plant and then on its fruit.

label: he | grasp | fruit
sentence: He grasped the fruit boldly.

label: he | say | people
sentence: He said to the people, “It is solid.

label: it | be | ∅
sentence: It is ripe.”

label: he | pull | husk
sentence: Then he pulled apart the husks, and said, “It is red.”

label: he | take | few
sentence: He took a few of the grains and showed them to the people.

label: he | eat | some
sentence: He ate some.

label: he | die | ∅
sentence: He did not die.

label: people | know | ∅
sentence: So the people knew Wahkoda had sent this plant to them for food.

label: leaf | turn | fall
sentence: Now in the fall, when the prairie grass turned brown, the leaves of this plant turned brown also.

label: fruit | pluck | ∅
sentence: Then the fruit was plucked and put away.

label: ∅ | divide | ∅
sentence: After the winter was over, the kernels were divided.

label: ∅ | be | four
sentence: There were four to each family.

label: people | move | lodge
sentence: Then the people moved the lodges to the place where the plant had grown.

label: they | plant | seed
sentence: When the hills became green, they planted the seed of the strange plant.

label: they | build | mound
sentence: But first they built little mounds like the one out of which it grew.

label: fruit | grow | ∅
sentence: So, the fruit grew and ripened.

label: it | have | color
sentence: It had many colors; red, and yellow, and white, and blue.

label: ∅ | be | plant
sentence: Then the next year there were many plants and many ears of corn.

label: they | send | ∅
sentence: So, they sent to other tribes.

label: they | invite | they
sentence: They invited them to visit them and gave them of the new food.

label: omahas | come | ∅
sentence: Thus, the Omahas came to have corn.
